questionText: Break down your organization's total gross global Scope 2 emissions by sector production activity in metric tons CO2e.
questionKo: 귀사의 total 전 세계 총 Scope 2 배출량 by sector production activity in 미터톤 CO2e.을/를 세분화하여 입력하십시오.
sectorTags: Oil & Gas, Coal, Aviation, Chemicals, Metals & mining, Transport services, Steel, Transport OEMS, Cement
simpleHelp: Reporting emissions by activity allows a more in-depth understanding of business risks related to future regulation and climate-related issues, and allows organizations to identify potential opportunities to reduce emissions associated with operational activities.

columns.text: Scope 2, market-based (if applicable), metric tons CO2e
columns.textKo: Scope 2, 시장기반 (만약 해당되는 경우), 미터톤 CO2e

guidance.text:
General 
This question requests gross global Scope 2 emissions (location- and market-based) by sector production activity, i.e. aggregated across all business divisions and/or facilities. 
This question is based on question 7.7 but is sector specific. Emissions deriving from the generation of electricity, steam, heat, and cooling that is purchased or acquired for consumption outside of your presented sector, should not be reported here. See the guidance in 7.19 for sector boundary definition. 
If your organization only operates within the presented sector, then the emissions figures you report here are still likely to be lower than the figures you reported in 7.7. This is because for this question CDP encourages you to exclude activities that are not dependent on being in the presented high-intensity sector. The purpose is to improve the consistency and accuracy of sector emissions reporting. 
If your organization operates in or owns assets across multiple sectors, then you should report emissions only for the presented sectors business division(s). Therefore, the figures you report here should be lower than the figures you reported in 7.7. 
If your organization is active across multiple high intensity sectors, complete this table as it is presented. 
Emissions must be reported in gross, not net figures. Therefore, negative numbers are not allowed. 
Emission figures should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 
If your organization imports electricity, steam, heat or cooling from an entity which is outside the sector boundary, but is nonetheless owned by the wider organization, then you should count for this here as a Scope 2 emission. Because the emissions occur outside of your Scope 1 boundary, they are Scope 2 emissions. 
Emissions estimates are acceptable, as long as there is transparency with regards to the estimation approach (what is estimated and how) and the data used for the analysis is adequate to support the objectives of the inventory. 
For more information on how to report Scope 2 emissions, see the Technical Note on "Accounting of Scope 2 emissions" , where you can find guidance on emission factors and the types that can be applied. Please also note that CH4 and N2O from electricity production should be included in the emissions factor calculation. 
For further information, see GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance . 
When accounting for your Scope 2 emissions, and should you need more information than provided in this guidance, you may want to consult your electricity suppliers, carbon advisor or verifier/assurer. 
Scope 2, location-based (metric tons CO2e) (column 2) 
Report your organization's total gross global Scope 2 emissions in CO2-equivalent for sector production activity listed in column 1, on a location-based method, i.e. reflecting the average emissions intensity of grids on which energy consumption occurs. 
Scope 2, market-based (if applicable), metric tons CO2e (column 3) 
Report your organization's total gross global Scope 2 emissions in CO2-equivalent for sector production activity listed in column 1, on a market-based method, i.e. reflecting emissions from electricity that companies have purposefully chosen (or their lack of choice). 
Comment (column 4) (optional) 
You can use this text field to enter any additional relevant information. 
Note for aviation sector 
Include emissions associated with activities in the aviation sector in row "Transport services activities".
Note for oil and gas sector 
This question splits oil and gas activities into upstream, midstream and downstream as follows: 
Rows will appear in this question corresponding to your selections in question 1.19
Upstream includes exploration, development, and production of oil and gas. 
Midstream includes the transportation, storage, and distribution of crude oil and natural gas. 
Downstream includes refining, processing, distribution, and marketing of products derived. For this question, CDP also includes 
Chemicals in this category, which includes the manufacture, distribution and marketing of chemical products derived from oil and gas (petrochemicals). 
Transport, storage, and distribution activities considered a defining part of the oil and gas sector value chain, e.g. midstream activities, should be included for oil and gas production activities. 
Projects considered an essential part of the oil and gas sector, such as exploration and extraction projects, should be included for oil and gas production activities.
guidance.textKo: 일반 This 문항 requests 전 세계 총 Scope 2 배출량 (location- 및 시장기반) by sector production activity, i.e. aggregated across all business divisions 및/또는 facilities. This 문항 is based on 문항 7.7 but is sector specific. Emissions deriving from the generation of electricity, steam, heat, 및 cooling that is purchased 또는 acquired for consumption outside of your presented sector, 해야 합니다 not be 보고 here. See the guidance in 7.19 for sector boundary definition. 만약 귀사 only operates within the presented sector, then the emissions figures you report here are still likely to be lower than the figures you 보고 in 7.7. This is because for this 문항 CDP encourages you to exclude activities that are not dependent on being in the presented high-intensity sector. The purpose is to improve the consistency 및 accuracy of sector emissions reporting. 만약 귀사 operates in 또는 owns assets across multiple sectors, then you 해야 합니다 report emissions only 에 대한 presented sectors business division(s). Therefore, the figures you report here 해야 합니다 be lower than the figures you 보고 in 7.7. 만약 귀사 is active across multiple high intensity sectors, complete this table as it is presented. Emissions 해야 합니다 be 보고 in gross, not net figures. Therefore, negative numbers are not allowed. Emission figures 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 보고연도 only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 만약 귀사 imports electricity, steam, heat 또는 cooling from an entity which is outside the sector boundary, but is nonetheless owned by the wider organization, then you 해야 합니다 count for this here as a Scope 2 emission. Because the emissions occur outside of your Scope 1 boundary, they are Scope 2 배출량. Emissions estimates are acceptable, as long as there is transparency with regards to the estimation approach (what is estimated 및 how) 및 the data used 에 대한 analysis is adequate to support the objectives 의 inventory. For more information on how to report Scope 2 배출량, see the Technical Note on "Accounting of Scope 2 배출량" , 여기서 you can find guidance on emission factors 및 the types that can be applied. Please also note that CH4 및 N2O from electricity production 해야 합니다 be included in the emissions factor calculation. For further information, see GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance . 경우 accounting 귀사의 Scope 2 배출량, 및 해야 합니다 you need more information than 입력 in this guidance, you may want to consult your electricity suppliers, carbon advisor 또는 verifier/assurer. Scope 2, 지역기반 (미터톤 CO2e) (열 2) Report 귀사의 total 전 세계 총 Scope 2 배출량 in CO2-equivalent for sector production activity listed in 열 1, on a 지역기반 method, i.e. reflecting the average 배출집약도 of grids on which 에너지 소비량 occurs. Scope 2, 시장기반 (만약 해당되는 경우), 미터톤 CO2e (열 3) Report 귀사의 total 전 세계 총 Scope 2 배출량 in CO2-equivalent for sector production activity listed in 열 1, on a 시장기반 method, i.e. reflecting emissions from electricity that companies have purposefully chosen (or their lack of choice). Comment (열 4) (optional) You can use this text field to enter 하나 이상 additional relevant information. Note for aviation sector Include emissions associated with activities in the aviation sector in 행 "Transport services activities". Note for oil 및 gas sector This 문항 splits oil 및 gas activities into upstream, midstream 및 downstream as follows: 행 will appear in this 문항 corresponding to your selections in 문항 1.19 Upstream includes exploration, development, 및 production of oil 및 gas. Midstream includes the transportation, storage, 및 distribution of crude oil 및 natural gas. Downstream includes refining, 프로세스ing, distribution, 및 marketing of products derived. For this 문항, CDP also includes Chemicals in this category, which includes the manufacture, distribution 및 marketing of chemical products derived from oil 및 gas (petrochemicals). Transport, storage, 및 distribution activities considered a defining part 의 oil 및 gas sector 가치사슬, e.g. midstream activities, 해야 합니다 be included for oil 및 gas production activities. Projects considered an essential part 의 oil 및 gas sector, such as exploration 및 extraction projects, 해야 합니다 be included for oil 및 gas production activities.

criteria.criteria:
ROUTE A)
Either 'We have no operations where...' OR 'We have operations where...' selected in column 'Scope 2, market-based' in question 7.3.
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed in column 'Scope 2, location-based, metric tons CO2e' completed.
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
'We are reporting a Scope 2, market-based figure' selected in column 'Scope 2, market-based' in question 7.3.
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed.
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this route.

criteria.criteria:
ROUTE A)
Either 'We have no operations where...' OR 'We have operations where...' selected in column 'Scope 2, market-based' in question 7.3.
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed in column 'Scope 2, location-based, metric tons CO2e' completed.
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
'We are reporting a Scope 2, market-based figure' selected in column 'Scope 2, market-based' in question 7.3.
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of cells displayed.
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this route.

sectorTagsKo: 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 항공, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 운송 OEM, 시멘트
simpleHelpKo: Reporting emissions by activity allows a more in-depth understanding of business risks related to future regulation 및 기후 관련 이슈, 및 allows organizations to identify potential opportunities to reduce emissions associated with operational activities.
